keyRules: A maximum of 120 m above ground level (EASA Open category)
Register as a UAS operator (€46.80, valid for 3 years) if the drone weighs 250 g or more or is equipped with a camera
A1/A3 online training required — minimum age 16
Mandatory liability insurance — at least 750,000 SDR in coverage

Vienna is located within the LO R 15 flight restriction zone: Flights are permitted only with a paid permit from Austro Control—except for drones weighing less than 250 g flying at a maximum altitude of 30 m above ground level.
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) with the drone
Never fly over people, crowds, or public events

Please note the geographic UAS areas (§ 18b LVR) on the Dronespace map—at airfields without an airfield zone, flying within the protection radius is permitted only outside of operating hours; airfield zones require a permit.
Night flights in the Open category are permitted with a flashing green light (EASA since 2022) — check specific zones on Dronespace.at
National Parks and Nature Reserves: Additional Restrictions Apply
importantNotes: Registration costs €46.80 (valid for 3 years) and is valid in all EU/EASA countries
Mandatory insurance with coverage of at least 750,000 SDR
Vienna (LO R 15): Permits are required for drones weighing 250 g or more—drones weighing less than 250 g do not require a permit only up to 30 m above ground level.
Alpine regions: Watch out for rescue helicopters' flight paths
The GDPR applies when recording identifiable individuals

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the official sources from Austro Control and Dronespace.at before every flight. This app is a tool—not a legal source. Austria follows EASA regulations with national additions.
